title: casselia davurica
intro: Casselia davurica, also known as Ferula davurica, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, particularly in China, Russia, and Korea. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Casselia davurica include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, making it a valuable remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. The herbal preparation of Casselia davurica typically involves drying and processing the plant's rhizome and roots, which are then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
